pr.title: Use fuzzy code % in Wiki TU page
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Tooling/documentation PR updating tools/wiki_tu.py so the Wiki TU page reports code progress using fuzzy code measures and fuzzy match percentages rather than strict matched_code measures. It also normalizes formatted byte strings with a bytes/byte regex and adds a float measure helper.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updates Wiki TU progress table calculations and size formatting.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: tools/wiki_tu.py added 16 deleted 9 hunks 5
raw_diff: diff --git a/tools/wiki_tu.py b/tools/wiki_tu.py

what_changed.text: Added BYTES_RE and changed friendly_size to replace singular or plural byte words with B via regex before converting spaces to non-breaking spaces.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: +BYTES_RE = re.compile(...)
raw_diff: friendly_size changed from .replace("bytes", "B") to BYTES_RE.sub("B", humanfriendly.format_size(value))

what_changed.text: Added get_measure_float(unit, key) to read numeric report measures as floats with a zero fallback.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: +def get_measure_float(unit, key: str) -> float

what_changed.text: Changed percent_cells text/code progress from matched_code and matched_code_percent to fuzzy_code and fuzzy_match_percent, while keeping data progress based on matched_data and matched_data_percent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: percent_cells uses get_measure_int(unit, "fuzzy_code") and get_measure_float(unit, "fuzzy_match_percent")
raw_diff: data percent uses get_measure_float(unit, "matched_data_percent")

what_changed.text: Changed full-row matched code display from matched_code to fuzzy_code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: row_full matched = friendly_size(get_measure_int(unit, "fuzzy_code"))

what_changed.text: For each report unit, computes fuzzy_code as int(total_code * fuzzy_match_percent / 100), stores it in unit["measures"], computes remaining_code from fuzzy_code, and classifies 100% fuzzy code units as data_left rather than in_progress unless metadata marks them complete.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fuzzy_percent = get_measure_float(unit, "fuzzy_match_percent")
raw_diff: fuzzy_code = int(total_code * fuzzy_percent / 100)
raw_diff: unit["measures"]["remaining_code"] = total_code - fuzzy_code
raw_diff: elif fuzzy_percent >= 100: data_left.append(unit)

smart_moves.text: The script derives a byte count for fuzzy_code from total_code and fuzzy_match_percent once per unit, then reuses that normalized measure for display and remaining-code calculation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fuzzy_code assignment and unit["measures"]["fuzzy_code"] update

smart_moves.text: A dedicated get_measure_float helper mirrors the existing get_measure_int helper and avoids repeated inline float conversion at call sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added get_measure_float and replaced direct float(unit["measures"].get(...)) usage

smart_moves.text: The byte-formatting change handles both byte and bytes output forms instead of only replacing the plural literal.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: BYTES_RE and friendly_size regex substitution

decomp_lessons.text: For progress-reporting tooling, decide whether a page is meant to show strict matched code or fuzzy-matched code; this PR makes the Wiki TU page code percentage and remaining-code display use fuzzy_match_percent/fuzzy_code.
evidence_refs: PR title: Use fuzzy code % in Wiki TU page
raw_diff: matched_code/matched_code_percent replaced with fuzzy_code/fuzzy_match_percent for text/code progress

naming_conventions.name: fuzzy_code
naming_conventions.meaning: A derived measure stored in unit["measures"] representing total_code scaled by fuzzy_match_percent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fuzzy_code = int(total_code * fuzzy_percent / 100)
raw_diff: unit["measures"]["fuzzy_code"] = fuzzy_code

naming_conventions.name: fuzzy_percent
naming_conventions.meaning: Local variable holding the float value of unit measure fuzzy_match_percent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fuzzy_percent = get_measure_float(unit, "fuzzy_match_percent")

naming_conventions.name: BYTES_RE
naming_conventions.meaning: Module-level regex used to normalize byte/bytes wording in humanfriendly.format_size output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: BYTES_RE = re.compile(...)
raw_diff: BYTES_RE.sub("B", humanfriendly.format_size(value))
